name: MVP scope monetization and risks
summary: Recommended MVP (macOS 13+, Apple Silicon first): menu bar start/stop; capture full screen OR frontmost app; interval + output speed presets; hardware H.264 MP4 export with segment recovery; screen recording permission UX. v2: idle skip, blacklist, multi-display, webcam PiP. Monetization: one-time license $19–29 or freemium (watermark free / Pro unlimited length + 4K) — mirrors Lapse BetaList promo patterns and avoids Screen Studio subscription fatigue.
key_findings: Building cross-platform doubles QA surface; App Store notarization and entitlements are launch gates; Commoditization risk from free TimeLapze; Support burden from OS updates; Privacy incidents if capture leaks wrong window
implications_for_trylapse: 8-week MVP scope: 3 capture modes, 2 presets, export validator, signed DMG; Dogfood with own build streams; Launch on HN/Product Hunt with color-accuracy or reliability story; Measure retention via second export within 7 days
strengths: Small team shippable scope with SCK; Clear upgrade path
weaknesses: Crowded niche; Free OSS ceiling on price; macOS-only limits TAM
metrics_or_signals: Lapse BetaList 50% promo indicates paid cross-platform exists
